post.summary: This API computes insights reports for the provided campaigns
post.description:
This API triggers computation of insights reports based on the provided request parameters including report type, campaign IDs, and date windows.
The computation process is asynchronous and returns a computation response with status and details.

---

#### Request Body Detail

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| reportType | String | **Type of report** to compute (e.g., PLD, NLD, ICT). Currently, VLD, SLS and AQS are not supported |
| campaignIds | List<Integer> | List of **campaign IDs** for which to compute insights reports. |
| startDate | Long | **Start date** timestamp for the report date window (epoch milliseconds). |
| endDate | Long | **End date** timestamp for the report date window (epoch milliseconds). |
| additionalParams | Map<String, Object> | **Optional parameters** specific to the report type. |

requestBody.description: Request body for computing insights reports.

errorObjects.error: One or more campaign IDs are invalid or not accessible
Date range is invalid or exceeds maximum allowed period

post.summary: Create report templates
post.description:
Creates a report template for the given campaigns and scheduling configuration for a particular insight report type provided.
It currently supports PLD, ICT and NLD.

The request defines:
- template name
- associated campaignIds
- frequency and frequencyDetails (daily / weekly / monthly / custom)
- template start/end dates
- aggregation flag
- recipient emails
- template status (ACTIVE / INACTIVE)
- category of reports

When the template is ACTIVE, the platform will use the configured schedule to trigger periodic report generation.

requestBody.description: Template definition with all supported scheduling patterns.

ONE_TIME.description: Runs only once at the given startDate.

DAILY.description: Runs every day between startDate and endDate.

WEEKLY.description: Runs every week on the given dayOfWeekId.

MONTHLY_FIRST_DAY.description: Runs on the 1st day of every month.

MONTHLY_LAST_DAY.description: Runs on the last day of every month.

CUSTOM_WEEKS.description: Runs every X weeks on a specific weekday.

CUSTOM_MONTHLY_FIRST_DAY.description: Runs every N months on the 1st day.

CUSTOM_MONTHLY_SPECIFIC_DAY.description: Runs every N months on a specific day.

CUSTOM_MONTHLY_LAST_DAY.description: Runs every N months on the last day.

value.name: Custom Every 2 Months – Last Day Template

400.description: Bad request due to missing or invalid inputs

errorObjects.error: startDate must be a positive epoch-millis value

422.description: Rule violation (for example: campaign status not allowed, IO mismatch)

errorObjects.error: All selected campaigns must belong to the same IO

get.summary: Retrieves the template details by template ID.
get.description: This API fetches the details of a template based on the provided template ID.

parameters.name: templateId
parameters.description: The ID of the template to retrieve.

patch.description:
Updates a template identified by templateId for the specified insight type.

This endpoint supports **partial updates**. Only the fields explicitly
present in the request are evaluated and applied.

The platform automatically re-evaluates **future executions**
based on the effective configuration after the update.

-------------------------
UPDATE BEHAVIOUR
-------------------------
Depending on the fields updated, the change may:

• affect only template metadata
• recalculate future execution dates
• pause or resume future executions
• trigger a one-time execution
• result in no further executions if the schedule is exhausted

Past executions and generated reports are never modified.

-------------------------
UPDATE SCENARIOS
-------------------------
| Scenario | Change Type                                   | Observable Effect                          |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| U1      | Metadata-only update                          | No impact to future executions             |
| U2      | Aggregation flag toggle                       | Future executions are recalculated         |
| U3      | End date modified                             | Future execution horizon is recalculated   |
| U4      | Start date modified                           | Future execution plan is recalculated      |
| U5a     | Recurring → Recurring (frequency change)     | Future execution pattern changes           |
| U5b     | Recurring → One-time                          | One-time execution is triggered            |
| U5c     | One-time → Recurring                          | Future recurring executions are scheduled  |
| U6      | ACTIVE → INACTIVE                             | All future executions are stopped          |
| U7      | INACTIVE → ACTIVE (recurring)                | Future executions resume                   |
| U8      | Multiple changes in one request              | Strongest applicable rule applies          |

-------------------------
IMPORTANT NOTES
-------------------------
• Updates only affect **future executions**
• A resumed template may not necessarily produce a future run
• ONE_TIME templates execute at most once
• If no future execution exists, nextRunDate will be null

requestBody.description:
PATCH payload containing only the fields to be updated.

Fields not present in the request remain unchanged.

FREQUENCY_CHANGE_DAILY_TO_WEEKLY.summary: Change frequency from daily to weekly

ONE_TIME_TO_RECURRING.summary: Convert one-time template to daily schedule

400.description: Bad request due to invalid PATCH fields

errorObjects.error: endDate must be greater than or equal to startDate
Template name already exists for this organization

404.description: Template not found or not accessible

errorObjects.error: Template does not exist or is not accessible

TemplateVO.description: Represents a report template with scheduling and configuration details

name.description: Template name visible to user. Must be unique for the advertiser.

campaignIds.description: List of campaign IDs selected by the user. Maximum 50 campaigns allowed.

items.description: List of campaign IDs selected by the user. Maximum 50 campaigns allowed.

startDate.description: Start date for the report data range in epoch milliseconds. Must be at least one day in the future for scheduled reports.

endDate.description: Optional end date for the report data range in epoch milliseconds. Must be on or after the start date.

recipientEmails.description: Optional list of explicit email recipients

items.description: Optional list of explicit email recipients

category.description: Category of insights report: PLD, ICT or NLD

TemplateRequest.description: Request payload for creating or updating a scheduling template

name.description: Template name to validate for uniqueness within the advertiser's account

category.description: Insight category/type for which the template is being created. Allowed values: PLD, ICT, NLD

TemplateNameValidationRequest.description: Request object for validating template name uniqueness within a given insight category, containing the template name and insight category

name.description: Template name visible to user. Must be unique for the advertiser.

recipientEmails.description: List of recipient email addresses for the template.

items.description: List of recipient email addresses for the template.

status.description: Status of the template. Allowed values: ACTIVE, INACTIVE

frequencyId.description: ID of the frequency for the template.

startDate.description: Start date of the template in epoch milliseconds.

endDate.description: End date of the template in epoch milliseconds.

aggregationEnabled.description: Indicates whether aggregation is enabled for the template.

TemplateUpdateRequest.description: Request object for updating a scheduling template, with PATCH semantics. Fields that are present in the JSON will override existing values; fields that are absent will preserve existing values; fields that are present with null value will clear the existing value.

nextRunDate.description: Next scheduled run date in epoch milliseconds (UTC). Null if the template is not active or schedule creation failed.

TemplateResponse.description: Response object returned after successful template creation, containing the template ID and optionally the next scheduled run date

data.description: List of reports generated from templates with their details

statusSummary.description: List of status summaries for the template reports.

frequencySummary.description: List of frequency summaries for the template reports.

TemplateReportsResponseVO.description: Response object for listing template reports with pagination and status/frequency summaries.
